ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of data center operations, the focus on capacity utilization metrics has become increasingly
vital for optimizing performance and ensuring operational efficiency. The analysis presented herein provides a comprehensive
overview of Tencent Holdings’ data center capacity utilization, underscoring key performance indicators that are critical for
industry stakeholders. Among these metrics are the TotalPower(MW) and UsedPower(MW), which play a crucial role in
understanding the operational capacity of our facilities. For instance, the facility with a TotalPower(MW) of 2000MW
demonstrates the importance of tracking the PowerUtilizationRatio, which is notably at 0 for this specific data point, indicating
that the data center is not currently utilizing any of its available power capacity. This scenario prompts an evaluation of the
underlying factors contributing to such low utilization rates, particularly in light of the competitive pressures facing the

technology and software sectors in Europe.

Moreover, the Performance Efficiency Ratio (PUE) is another critical metric
included in our analysis. For the aforementioned 2000MW facility, the PUE stands at an impressive 70.0%, indicating that the
data center operates with remarkable efficiency relative to its energy consumption and cooling requirements. This is particularly
noteworthy in an era where energy efficiency is paramount, as organizations are increasingly held accountable for their
environmental impact.
Metrics  CapacityUtilizationMetrics
TotalCapacity  UsedCapacity  UtilizationRatio  AverageUtilization  PeakUtilization
DataCenterCount  NewCenters  RetiredCenters  TotalArea(sqm)  UtilizationIndex  PowerEfficiencyMetrics
TotalPower(MW)  UsedPower(MW)  PowerUtilizationRatio  PUE  AveragePUE  PeakPUE  CoolingEfficiency  TotalRevenue

50000  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  1.5  1.6  1.4  1.8  [HIDDEN]
The CoolingEfficiency metric highlights that this specific center maintains a cooling efficiency rating of 2, further emphasizing

the commitment to sustainable practices within the industry.

From a financial perspective, understanding the relationship
between capacity utilization and revenue generation is essential. In this report, we detail the TotalRevenue, which has reached
an impressive figure of 100MEUR, reflecting the economic viability of our operations despite the currently unused power
capacity. This juxtaposition of high revenue against a backdrop of low UsedPower(MW) illustrates the potential for revenue
optimization through enhanced utilization strategies. It is evident that maximizing UsedPower(MW) while maintaining

efficiency will be crucial for future growth and competitiveness.

In summary, the data center capacity utilization metrics
provide a multifaceted view of operational effectiveness and financial performance. The combination of TotalPower(MW),
UsedPower(MW), PowerUtilizationRatio, PUE, and CoolingEfficiency not only serves to benchmark current performance but
also guides strategic decisions moving forward. These insights are instrumental in shaping our approach to capacity
management and operational efficiency, ensuring that Tencent Holdings remains at the forefront of the technology and software
industry in Europe. The findings presented in this report will inform stakeholders of the current status while highlighting areas
for improvement, ultimately aiming to enhance the overall performance of our data centers.
